Definition (EN): An occurrence definition is a definition of a class of occurrences that have an extent in time and may have spatial extent.
Context/Example (EN): An occurrence definition is a definition of a class of occurrences that have an extent in time and may have spatial extent.

Definition (EN): An occurrence definition is a definition of a class of occurrences that have an extent in time and may have spatial extent.
Context/Example (EN): An occurrence definition is a definition of a class of occurrences that have an extent in time and may have spatial extent.

Definition (EN): An occurrence usage is a usage of an occurrence definition.
Context/Example (EN): An occurrence usage is a usage of an occurrence definition.

Equivalent (JP): ライフタイム / 生存期間
Definition (EN): The extent of an occurrence in time is known as its lifetime, which covers the period in time from the occurrence's creation to its destruction.
Context/Example (EN): The extent of an occurrence in time is known as its lifetime, which covers the period in time from the occurrence's creation to its destruction.

Equivalent (JP): タイムスライス / 時間区分
Definition (EN): The lifetime of an occurrence can be partitioned into time slices which correspond to some duration of time. These time slices represent periods or phases of a lifetime, such as the deployment or operational phase.
Context/Example (EN): The lifetime of an occurrence can be partitioned into time slices which correspond to some duration of time.

Definition (EN): A time slice with zero duration is a snapshot. Start, end and intermediate snapshots can be defined for any time slice to represent particular instants of time in an occurrence's lifetime.
Context/Example (EN): A time slice with zero duration is a snapshot.

Definition (EN): Any kind of occurrence definition can be restricted to define a class that represents an individual, that is, a single real or perceived object with a unique identity.
Context/Example (EN): Any kind of occurrence definition can be restricted to define a class that represents an individual, that is, a single real or perceived object with a unique identity.

Definition (EN): An occurrence definition (of any kind) can be declared as an individual definition using the keyword individual, placed immediately before the kind keyword of the declaration.
An event is a reference from one occurrence to another occurrence that represents some relevant happening during the lifetime of the first occurrence.
Context/Example (EN): An event is a reference from one occurrence to another occurrence that represents some relevant happening during the lifetime of the first occurrence.

Definition (EN): An event occurrence usage is declared like an occurrence usage, as described in 7.9.2, 7.9.3, and 7.9.4, but using the kind keyword event occurrence instead of just occurrence.
Context/Example (EN): An event occurrence usage is declared like an occurrence usage, as described in 7.9.2, 7.9.3, and 7.9.4, but using the kind keyword event occurrence instead of just occurrence.

Equivalent (JP): タイムスライス (キーワード)
Definition (EN): An occurrence usage (of any kind) can be declared as a time slice or a snapshot using the keyword timeslice or snapshot, respectively, placed immediately before the kind keyword of the declaration (after any of the other usage property keywords described in 7.6.3).

Equivalent (JP): スナップショット (キーワード)
Definition (EN): An occurrence usage (of any kind) can be declared as a time slice or a snapshot using the keyword timeslice or snapshot, respectively, placed immediately before the kind keyword of the declaration (after any of the other usage property keywords described in 7.6.3).

Equivalent (JP): イベント事象 (キーワード)
Definition (EN): An event occurrence usage may also be declared using just the keyword event instead of event occurrence. In this case, the declaration does not include either a name or a short name.
